body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Update the signature of `GenerateCoreAsync` to accept the dynamic `batesStartOverride` and an `Action<int> onPlansGenerated` callback to track the actual number of Bates numbers consumed.

```c#
    private static async Task<ProductionSetResult> GenerateCoreAsync(
        FileGenerationRequest request,
        string productionPath,
        string productionName,
        int rollingIndex,
        long batesStartOverride,
        Action<int> onPlansGenerated,
        Stopwatch stopwatch,
        CancellationToken cancellationToken)
```

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Update `ProductionSetPlanner.Plan` signature to accept an optional `overrideBatesStart` parameter.

```c#
    public static IReadOnlyList<ProductionNativeFilePlan> Plan(FileGenerationRequest request, int rollingIndex = 0, long? overrideBatesStart = null)
```

_📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Remove dead assignment to `currentStart` on line 125.**

`currentStart = start;` is immediately overwritten by `currentStart = start + fileCount;` on line 131, making the assignment and the `// sync` comment dead code. This is confirmed by SonarCloud.





<details>
<summary>🧹 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
                     if (parsed.BatesStarts is not null && parsed.BatesStarts.Count > i)
                     {
                         start = parsed.BatesStarts[i];
-                        currentStart = start; // sync
                     }
                     else
                     {
                         start = currentStart;
                     }
                     currentStart = start + fileCount;
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
                    if (parsed.BatesStarts is not null && parsed.BatesStarts.Count > i)
                    {
                        start = parsed.BatesStarts[i];
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        start = currentStart;
                    }
                    currentStart = start + fileCount;
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🧰 Tools</summary>

<details>
<summary>🪛 GitHub Check: SonarCloud Code Analysis</summary>

[warning] 125-125: Remove this useless assignment to local variable 'currentStart'.

See more on https://sonarcloud.io/project/issues?id=dwojtaszek_zipper&issues=AZ9IhgDaiwhH8Ert4CQL&open=AZ9IhgDaiwhH8Ert4CQL&pullRequest=587

</details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@src/Cli/Validation/ProductionSetValidator.cs` around lines 122 - 131, In
ProductionSetValidator’s Bates start calculation, remove the redundant sync
assignment to currentStart inside the parsed.BatesStarts branch because it is
immediately overwritten later in the same block. Keep the logic in the
surrounding loop intact, and rely on the final currentStart = start + fileCount
update as the only state change so the dead assignment and its comment are
eliminated.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-indicator-types:potential_issue -->

<!-- cr-comment:v1:510c0fa1f84aaef0abd2da3a -->

_Source: Linters/SAST tools_

diff_hunk:
@@ -47,6 +50,11 @@ public static async Task<string> WriteAsync(
         var manifest = new ProductionManifest
         {
             ProductionDate = DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ", System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),
+            ProductionId = productionId ?? Path.GetFileName(productionPath),
+            RollingSequenceNumber = rollingSequenceNumber,
+            BatesNumberStart = batesStart,
+            BatesNumberEnd = batesEnd,
+            BatesRangeMode = batesRangeMode ?? "continuous",
body:
_🗄️ Data Integrity & Integration_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**`BatesRange.Prefix` still uses the base prefix**

`BatesRange.Start`/`End` use the resolved per-set Bates numbers, but `BatesRange.Prefix` still reads `request.Bates?.Prefix`. When rolling `--bates-prefix` values are used, the manifest can mix `"PROD00200000006"` with prefix `"PROD"`, which leaves the manifest internally inconsistent.

Pass the resolved per-set prefix into `WriteAsync` and use that value for `BatesRange.Prefix`.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@src/ProductionManifestWriter.cs` around lines 53 - 57, BatesRange.Prefix is
still coming from the base request prefix instead of the resolved per-set
prefix, so the manifest can end up inconsistent with the generated Bates
numbers. Update ProductionManifestWriter.WriteAsync to accept the resolved
prefix for each set, and use that value when assigning BatesRange.Prefix
alongside the existing BatesRange.Start and BatesRange.End values. Make the
change where the manifest object is built so the per-set prefix is propagated
consistently.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-indicator-types:potential_issue -->
